Title: Facing your fears
ChapterText:
Think of it as 'brain training.' When we avoid what scares us, our anxiety grows. When we gently lean into it, our brain learns a new truth: 'I am uncomfortable, but I am safe.'
When you consistently face your fears your brain habituates to it and the level of anxiety decreases. Many times you will realize, that your fear was exaggerated and people react to you better than you thought before.

Title: Exposure therapy
ChapterText: Exposure therapy is part of CBT and is the gold standard for overcoming social anxiety. It's about taking small, manageable steps to face the situations you dread, proving to your brain that you are safer than you feel.

Title: Create your fear ladder
ChapterText:
When applying exposure therapy, you have to first create a list of  you are scared of. The list goes from the situations you worry about the least to the most scary situations. The scariest situation can be for example: "Going on a date" while the least scary situation could be: "Holding an eye contact with strangers "

Now take a piece of paper and a pen, and write a list of situations you dread. From the least scary to the most...

Title: Take action
ChapterText:
Theory is the foundation, but action is where the change happens. Pick a small challenge today and show your anxiety who’s in charge. Don't worry if the situation feel harder in reality than on paper. Your goal is to step to the action despite the fear.

After overcoming your fear, don't forget to reward yourself. Not only you will feel more motivated to overcome your fear next time but you will also learn to be more kind to yourself and appreciate yourself

Title: Courage is a Muscle
ChapterText:
Exposure isn't about getting rid of anxiety—it's about realizing that anxiety can't stop you. By facing social situations in small doses, you are rewiring your nervous system and reclaiming your freedom.
One step at a time.
